description: Three short interruption experiments for manual alignment.

cases.title: Corrective interruption
cases.description: Speaker B corrects Speaker A and takes the floor.

speaker_a.text: We could take the train from the central station, because the last one leaves at around...
speaker_a.instruction: A casually confident young adult speaking naturally to a friend at a medium conversational pace. The speaker is explaining a simple travel plan. Near the end, become less certain and trail off gently, as though another person has begun correcting the statement. Keep the delivery subtle and ordinary.

speaker_b.text: No, they moved it to the north station.
speaker_b.instruction: A clear conversational young adult voice entering quickly to correct an important detail. Speak slightly faster than normal with mild urgency, confidence, and friendliness. Begin decisively without hesitation.

cases.alignment_notes: Try starting B 100 to 300 ms before A ends.

cases.title: Enthusiastic collaborative interruption
cases.description: Speaker B recognizes and completes Speaker A's thought with excitement.

speaker_a.text: I found this tiny restaurant near the river, and apparently they make their own...
speaker_a.instruction: A warm, animated young adult speaking informally to a friend. Sound genuinely pleased about a recent discovery. Use a natural medium pace and trail off with positive energy near the end.

speaker_b.text: The fresh pasta! I've been there.
speaker_b.instruction: A spontaneous, energetic young adult voice suddenly recognizing what the other speaker means. Enter quickly with genuine excitement and warmth. Emphasize fresh pasta, then relax slightly.

cases.alignment_notes: Try starting B just before A's final word fully resolves.

cases.title: Clarifying interruption
cases.description: Speaker B jumps in with a quick clarification before Speaker A finishes the condition.

speaker_a.text: If the client signs the revised contract today, then we should probably schedule the kickoff for...
speaker_a.instruction: A focused project lead speaking in a calm meeting voice. Start steadily and sound practical. Near the end, slow slightly and trail off as if someone has entered with a useful clarification. Keep it conversational, not dramatic.

speaker_b.text: Tuesday, after legal sends the countersigned copy.
speaker_b.instruction: A concise colleague entering quickly with a helpful clarification. Speak with calm confidence, a little faster than normal, and sound collaborative rather than forceful.

cases.alignment_notes: Try starting B 150 to 350 ms before A ends, then compare whether B sounds helpful or too abrupt.
